reformat_parsed_response.translation: Then the Venerable Sāriputta approached the Blessed One. Having approached and paid homage to the Blessed One, he sat down to one side. As the Venerable Sāriputta was sitting to one side, the Blessed One said to him: "Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma in brief, Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma in detail, or, Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma both in brief and in detail, but those who understand are hard to find."
reformat_parsed_response.literal_translation: Then indeed the venerable Sāriputta where the Blessed One there approached, having approached the Blessed One having paid homage to one side sat down. To one side seated indeed to the venerable Sāriputta the Blessed One this said, in brief also indeed I, Sāriputta, Dhamma would teach, in detail also indeed I, Sāriputta, Dhamma would teach, in brief and detail also indeed I, Sāriputta, Dhamma would teach, understanders and are rare.

### Common Pāḷi Disambiguation Rules:
- In the stock phrase `kāyassa bhedā paraṃ maraṇā`, `kāyassa` is genitive, `bhedā` and `maraṇā` are ablative singular, and `paraṃ` is the indeclinable preposition "after" — never nominative plurals.
- Final-vowel lengthening before quotative `'ti` is sandhi: prefer the deconstruction restoring the short final vowel (e.g., `upapajjanti + iti`) at the end of a quotation unless context clearly requires a long-vowel reading.
- In `yena <person/place> tena upasaṅkami`, `yena` and `tena` are adverbial "where ... there" rows, not plain instrumental pronouns.
- Inside direct speech, a comma-set-off word addressing the listener (e.g., `bho` or a teacher's name) is usually vocative.
- Counted time-spans such as `paṇṇavīsativassāni` / `vassāni` ("for twenty-five years") are accusative of duration, not nominative.
- When dative and genitive variants of the same surface form are offered, prefer the genitive for possession or relation ("of X"); select dative only when the context expresses a recipient, purpose, or benefit ("for/to X").

Then the Venerable Sāriputta approached the Blessed One. Having approached and paid homage to the Blessed One, he sat down to one side. As the Venerable Sāriputta was sitting to one side, the Blessed One said to him: "Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma in brief, Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma in detail, or, Sāriputta, I could teach the Dhamma both in brief and in detail, but those who understand are hard to find."

translation_parsed_response.translation: Now is the time for this, Blessed One, now is the time for this, Sublime One, that the Blessed One should teach the Dhamma in brief, teach the Dhamma in detail, and teach the Dhamma both in brief and in detail. There will be those who understand the Dhamma.
translation_parsed_response.literal_translation: Of this, Blessed One, [is] the time, of this, Sublime One, [is] the time, which the Blessed One in brief even the Dhamma should teach, in detail even the Dhamma should teach, in brief and detail even the Dhamma should teach. There will be of the Dhamma understanders.

### Common Pāḷi Disambiguation Rules:
- In the stock phrase `kāyassa bhedā paraṃ maraṇā`, `kāyassa` is genitive, `bhedā` and `maraṇā` are ablative singular, and `paraṃ` is the indeclinable preposition "after" — never nominative plurals.
- Final-vowel lengthening before quotative `'ti` is sandhi: prefer the deconstruction restoring the short final vowel (e.g., `upapajjanti + iti`) at the end of a quotation unless context clearly requires a long-vowel reading.
- In `yena <person/place> tena upasaṅkami`, `yena` and `tena` are adverbial "where ... there" rows, not plain instrumental pronouns.
- Inside direct speech, a comma-set-off word addressing the listener (e.g., `bho` or a teacher's name) is usually vocative.
- Counted time-spans such as `paṇṇavīsativassāni` / `vassāni` ("for twenty-five years") are accusative of duration, not nominative.
- When dative and genitive variants of the same surface form are offered, prefer the genitive for possession or relation ("of X"); select dative only when the context expresses a recipient, purpose, or benefit ("for/to X").

### Common Pāḷi Disambiguation Rules:
- In the stock phrase `kāyassa bhedā paraṃ maraṇā`, `kāyassa` is genitive, `bhedā` and `maraṇā` are ablative singular, and `paraṃ` is the indeclinable preposition "after" — never nominative plurals.
- Final-vowel lengthening before quotative `'ti` is sandhi: prefer the deconstruction restoring the short final vowel (e.g., `upapajjanti + iti`) at the end of a quotation unless context clearly requires a long-vowel reading.
- In `yena <person/place> tena upasaṅkami`, `yena` and `tena` are adverbial "where ... there" rows, not plain instrumental pronouns.
- Inside direct speech, a comma-set-off word addressing the listener (e.g., `bho` or a teacher's name) is usually vocative.
- Counted time-spans such as `paṇṇavīsativassāni` / `vassāni` ("for twenty-five years") are accusative of duration, not nominative.
- When dative and genitive variants of the same surface form are offered, prefer the genitive for possession or relation ("of X"); select dative only when the context expresses a recipient, purpose, or benefit ("for/to X").

options.example_1:
okaṃ pahāya aniketasārī,
gāme akubbaṃ muni santhavāni,
kāmehi ritto apurekkharāno,
kathaṃ na viggayha janena kayirā.

na brāhmaṇo kappam'upeti saṅkhā,
na diṭṭhisārī na'pi ñāṇabandhu,
ñatvā ca so sammutiyo puthujjā,
upekkhatī uggahaṇanti m'aññe.
options.example_2:
attaṃ pahāya anupādiyāno,
ñāṇe'pi so nissayaṃ no karoti,
sa ve viyuttesu na vaggasārī,
diṭṭhim'pi so na pacceti kiñci.

options.example_1:
saccaṃ dhammo saṃyamo brahmacariyaṃ,
majjhe sitā brāhmaṇa brahmapatti,
sa t'ujjubhūtesu namo karohi,
tam'ahaṃ naraṃ dhammasārī'ti brūmī'ti.

mātā yathā niyaṃ puttaṃ,
āyusā ekaputtam'anurakkhe,
evam'pi sabbabhūtesu,
mānasaṃ bhāvaye aparimāṇaṃ.
options.example_2:
na tassa puttā pasavo,
khettaṃ vatthuñ'ca vijjati,
attā vā'pi nirattā vā,
na tasmiṃ upalabbhati.

options.meaning_1: thereby; because of that; on that account
options.meaning_combo: thereby; because of that; on that account; lit. by that; with that
options.example_1: tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā tesaṃ'y'eva pāpakānaṃ akusalānaṃ dhammānaṃ pahānāya adhimatto chando ca vāyāmo ca ussāho ca ussoḷhī ca appaṭivānī ca sati ca sampajaññañ'ca karaṇīyaṃ.
options.example_2:
yo bālo maññati bālyaṃ,
paṇḍito vā'pi tena so,
bālo ca paṇḍitamānī,
sa ve bālo'ti vuccati.

options.grammar: ind, adv, instr sg of ta
options.example_1:
yena yena subhikkhāni,
sivāni abhayāni ca,
tena puttaka gacchassu,
mā sokāpahato bhavā'ti.
options.example_2: atha kho ambaṭṭho māṇavo yena so vihāro saṃvutadvāro, tena appasaddo upasaṅkamitvā ataramāno āḷindaṃ pavisitvā ukkāsitvā aggaḷaṃ ākoṭesi. vivari bhagavā dvāraṃ.

options.meaning_combo: approached; drew near; went (to); lit. went near

options.grammar: ind, adv, comp, acc sg of ekamanta
options.example_1: atha kho bhagavā kumbhakār'āvesanaṃ pavisitvā ekam'antaṃ tiṇasanthārakaṃ paññāpetvā nisīdi pallaṅkaṃ ābhujitvā ujuṃ kāyaṃ paṇidhāya parimukhaṃ satiṃ upaṭṭhapetvā.
options.example_2: tena kho pana samayena kasibhāradvājassa brāhmaṇassa parivesanā vattati. atha kho bhagavā yena parivesanā ten'upasaṅkami, upasaṅkamitvā ekam'antaṃ aṭṭhāsi.

options.grammar: ind, adv, comp, acc sg of ekamanta

options.example_1: seyyathā'pi, bhikkhave, mahāsamuddo ekaraso loṇaraso, evam'eva kho, bhikkhave, ayaṃ dhammavinayo ekaraso vimuttiraso
options.example_2:
yo ca gāthā sataṃ bhāse,
anatthapadasaṃhitā,
ekaṃ dhammapadaṃ seyyo,
yaṃ sutvā upasammati.

options.example_1:
yo ca vassasataṃ jantu,
aggiṃ paricare vane,
ekañ'ca bhāvit'attānaṃ,
muhuttam'api pūjaye,
sā'y'eva pūjanā seyyo,
yañ'ce vassasataṃ hutaṃ.
options.example_2: atīte kira kassapasammāsambuddhakāle gāmavāsī eko kuṭumbiko ekassa therassa vihāraṃ katvā taṃ tattha viharantaṃ catūhi paccayehi upaṭṭhahi

options.example_1:
supupphit'aggaṃ upagamma bhikkhuni,
ekā tuvaṃ tiṭṭhasi sālamūle,
na c'atthi te dutiyā vaṇṇadhātu,
bāle na tvaṃ bhāyasi dhuttakānan'ti.
options.example_2: so eko gāmaṃ piṇḍāya pavisati eko paṭikkamati eko raho nisīdati eko caṅkamaṃ adhiṭṭhāti.

options.example_1: tesam'assa ekaputtako piyo manāpo
options.example_2:
mātā yathā niyaṃ puttaṃ āyusā ekaputtam'anurakkhe, 
evam'pi sabbabhūtesu,
mānasam'bhāvaye aparimāṇaṃ.

options.example_1: yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṃ saṃvidhāya ek'addhānamaggaṃ paṭipajjeyya antamaso gām'antaram'pi aññatra samayā, pācittiyaṃ. tatth'āyaṃ samayo, satthagamanīyo hoti maggo, sāsaṅkasammato, sappaṭibhayo, ayaṃ tattha samayo.
options.example_2: yo pana bhikkhu bhikkhuniyā saddhiṃ saṃvidhāya ekaṃ nāvaṃ abhiruheyya uddhaṃgāminiṃ vā adhogāminiṃ vā aññatra tiriyaṃ taraṇāya, pācittiyaṃ.

options.meaning_1: one thing; single thing; a certain thing
options.meaning_combo: one thing; single thing; a certain thing
options.example_1: ye āsavā saṃkilesikā ponobbhavikā sadarā dukkhavipākā āyatiṃ jātijarāmaraṇiyā, appahīnā te tathāgatassa, tasmā tathāgato saṅkhāy'ekaṃ paṭisevati, saṅkhāy'ekaṃ adhivāseti, saṅkhāy'ekaṃ parivajjeti, saṅkhāy'ekaṃ vinodetī'ti?
yattha nu kho, bhante, na jāyati na jīyati na mīyati na cavati na upapajjati, sakkā nu kho so, bhante, gamanena lokassa anto ñātuṃ vā daṭṭhuṃ vā pāpuṇituṃ vā'ti?
options.example_2:
es'ūpamā dāmali brāhmaṇassa,
khīṇ'āsavassa nipakassa jhāyino,
pappuyya jātimaraṇassa antaṃ,
n'āyūhatī pāragato hi so'ti.

options.example_1:
ubhosu antesu vineyya chandaṃ,
phassaṃ pariññāya anānugiddho,
yadattagarahī tadakubbamāno,
na lippatī diṭṭhasutesu dhīro.
options.example_2: evaṃ vutte aññataro bhikkhu there bhikkhū etadavoca, phasso kho, āvuso, eko anto, phassasamudayo dutiyo anto, phassanirodho majjhe, taṇhā sibbinī.

options.meaning_1: ending up as; finishing in; resulting in
options.meaning_combo: ending up as; finishing in; resulting in
options.example_1: kāpotakāni aṭṭhīni bhavanti. bhass'antā āhutiyo, dattupaññattaṃ yadidaṃ dānaṃ. tesaṃ tucchā musā vilāpo ye keci atthikavādaṃ vadanti.
atthi imasmiṃ kāye… antaṃ antaguṇaṃ udariyaṃ karīsaṃ (matthaluṅgaṃ), pittaṃ semhaṃ pubbo lohitaṃ sedo medo, assu vasā kheḷo siṅghāṇikā lasikā muttan'ti.
options.example_2: atha kho so, bhikkhave, mudumūsi gocarāya pakkāmi. tam'enaṃ biḷāro gahetvā sahasā saṅkhāditvā ajjhohari. tassa so mudumūsi antam'pi khādi, antaguṇam'pi khādi.

options.example_1: sañcetanikā sukkavissaṭṭhi aññatra supin'antā saṅgh'ādiseso.
options.example_2: evaṃ, bhante. sace so, bhikkhave, dārukkhandho na orimaṃ tīraṃ upagacchati, na pārimaṃ tīraṃ upagacchati, na majjhe saṃsīdissati, na thale ussīdissati, na manussaggāho gahessati, na amanussaggāho gahessati, na āvaṭṭaggāho gahessati, na antopūti bhavissati, evañ'hi so, bhikkhave, dārukkhandho samuddaninno bhavissati samuddapoṇo samuddapabbhāro. taṃ kissa hetu?

options.example_1:
okaṃ pahāya aniketasārī,
gāme akubbaṃ muni santhavāni,
kāmehi ritto apurekkharāno,
kathaṃ na viggayha janena kayirā.

na diṭṭhisārī na'pi ñāṇabandhu,

sa ve viyuttesu na vaggasārī,
diṭṭhim'pi so na pacceti kiñci.

options.example_1:
anejaṃ mūladassāviṃ,
atthi pañhena āgamaṃ,
kiṃ nissitā isayo manujā,
khattiyā brāhmaṇā devatānaṃ,
yaññam'akappayiṃsu puthū'dha loke,
pucchāmi taṃ bhagavā brūhi me taṃ.
options.example_2: bhagavaṃmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā bhagavaṃnettikā bhagavaṃpaṭisaraṇā. sādhu vata, bhante, bhagavantaṃ'y'eva paṭibhātu etassa bhāsitassa attho. bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī'ti.

options.meaning_combo: (vinaya) appoints; allocates; designates (to); lit. causes to point out
options.example_1: so saṅghaṃ kuṭivatthudesanaṃ yācati. yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṃ, saṅgho itthan'nāmassa bhikkhuno kuṭivatthuṃ deseyya. esā ñatti.

options.meaning_combo: extensive; detailed; broad; full; lit. spread out
options.example_1: vitthār'uddeso pañcamo bhikkhupātimokkhaṃ niṭṭhitaṃ.

options.meaning_combo: extensive; detailed; broad; full; lit. spread out
options.example_1: vitthār'uddeso pañcamo bhikkhupātimokkhaṃ niṭṭhitaṃ.
dīghato tiṃsayojanaṃ vitthārato pannarasayojanaṃ assamapadaṃ māpehī'ti āha

options.meaning_combo: detailed description; extended explanation; lit. spread out
options.example_1: katamo ca, gahapatayo, puggalo attan'tapo attaparitāpan'ānuyogam'anuyutto? idha, gahapatayo, ekacco puggalo acelako hoti mutt'ācāro hatth'āpalekhano… [vitthāro ma. ni. 2.6-7 kandarakasutte]

options.grammar: ind, adv, instr sg of vitthāra
options.meaning_combo: in detail; in full; thoroughly; lit. by spread out
options.example_1: kathañ'ca, thera, ekavihāro vitthārena paripuṇṇo hoti. idha, thera, yaṃ atītaṃ taṃ pahīnaṃ, yaṃ anāgataṃ taṃ paṭinissaṭṭhaṃ, paccuppannesu ca attabhāvapaṭilābhesu chandarāgo suppaṭivinīto.
options.example_2: imassa nu kho, sāriputta, saṃkhittena bhāsitassa kathaṃ vitthārena attho daṭṭhabbo'ti? evaṃ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto tuṇhī ahosi.

options.grammar: ind, adv, instr sg of vitthāra
options.meaning_combo: in length; in height; lit. by spread out
options.example_1: ayaṃ me, bhante, bāhitikā raññā māgadhena ajātasattunā vedehiputtena vatthanāḷiyā pakkhipitvā pahitā soḷasasamā āyāmena, aṭṭhasamā vitthārena. taṃ, bhante, āyasmā ānando paṭiggaṇhātu anukampaṃ upādāyā'ti.

options.grammar: ind, adv, instr sg of vitthāra
options.meaning_combo: from north to south; lit. by spread out
options.example_1: rañño, ānanda, mahāsudassanassa ayaṃ kusinārā kusāvatī nāma rājadhānī ahosi, puratthimena ca pacchimena ca dvādasayojanāni āyāmena, uttarena ca dakkhiṇena ca sattayojanāni vitthārena.

bahussuto ca medhāvī,
sīlesu susamāhito,
cetosamatham'anuyutto,
api muddhani tiṭṭhatu.

api dibbesu kāmesu,
ratiṃ so n'ādhigacchati,
taṇhakkhayarato hoti,
sammāsambuddhasāvako.
options.example_2:
disvāna taṇhaṃ aratiṃ ragañ'ca,
n'āhosi chando api methunasmiṃ,
kim'ev'idaṃ muttakarīsapuṇṇaṃ,
pādā'pi naṃ samphusituṃ na icche.

options.example_1: sacc'āhaṃ, ācariya, paṭissuṇiṃ, api-ahaṃ marissāmi, n'āhaṃ sakkomi ekena passena sattamāse nipajjitun'ti
options.example_2:
yathā'pi mūle anupaddave daḷhe,
chinno'pi rukkho punareva rūhati, 
evam'pi taṇh'ānusaye anūhate,
nibbattatī dukkham'idaṃ punappunaṃ.

options.example_1: api, samaṇa, balībadde addasā'ti?
options.example_2: addasā kho aññataro upāsako taṃ bhikkhuṃ kīṭāgirismiṃ piṇḍāya carantaṃ. disvāna yena so bhikkhu ten'upasaṅkami, upasaṅkamitvā taṃ bhikkhuṃ abhivādetvā etadavoca, api, bhante, piṇḍo labbhatī'ti?

options.example_1: api ca, udāyi, ānando diṭṭh'eva dhamme parinibbāyissatī'ti
yam'pi taṃ diṭṭhaṃ sutaṃ mutaṃ viññātaṃ pattaṃ pariyesitaṃ, anuvicaritaṃ manasā tam'pi etaṃ mama, eso'ham'asmi, eso me attā'ti samanupassati
so tena ñattena attān'ukkaṃseti, paraṃ vambheti. ayam'pi, bhikkhave, asappurisadhammo.
options.example_2: jāti'pi dukkhā, jarā'pi dukkhā, maraṇam'pi dukkhaṃ, soka-parideva-dukkha-domanass'upāyāsā'pi dukkhā

options.meaning_combo: brief; concise; contracted; abbreviated; lit. thrown together
options.example_1: sādhu me bhante bhagavā saṃkhittena dhammaṃ desetu
options.example_2: ko nu kho imassa bhagavatā saṃkhittena uddesassa uddiṭṭhassa vitthārena atthaṃ avibhattassa vitthārena atthaṃ vibhajeyyā'ti?
